hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2 ECL2 residues (e.g., Y193) and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2-specific interactions while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to consistently produce SSTR2-selective ligands. A more focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions and a stricter ddG threshold are necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is not stringent enough to filter out candidates with weak binding or poor selectivity. Raising the ddG threshold will prioritize stronger SSTR2 binders.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved interactions in off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5) at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions can reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
